00026  * OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF
00027  * SUCH DAMAGE.

00029 
00030 /*
00031  * hash: Takes a file and computes a "hash" value by adding together all
00032  * the values in the file mod some largish prime.
00033  *
00034  * Once the basic system calls are complete, this should work on any
00035  * file the system supports. However, it's probably of most use for
00036  * testing your file system code.
00037  *
00038  * This should really be replaced with a real hash, like MD5 or SHA-1.
00039  */
00040 
00041 #include <stdio.h>
00042 #include <unistd.h>
00043 #include <fcntl.h>
00044 #include <err.h>
00045 
00046 #ifdef HOST
00047 #include "hostcompat.h"
00048 #endif
00049 
00050 #define HASHP 104729
00051 
00052 int
00053 main(int argc, char *argv[])
00054 {
00055         int fd;
00056         char readbuf[1];
00057         int j = 0;
00058 
00059 #ifdef HOST
00060         hostcompat_init(argc, argv);
00061 #endif
00062 
00063         if (argc != 2) {
00064                 errx(1, "Usage: hash filename");
00065         }
00066         
00067         fd = open(argv[1], O_RDONLY, 0664);
00068         
00069         if (fd<0) { 
00070                 err(1, "%s", argv[1]);
00071         }
00072 
00073         for (;;) {
00074                 if (read(fd, readbuf, 1) <= 0) break;
00075                 j = ((j*8) + (int) readbuf[0]) % HASHP;
00076         }
00077 
00078         close(fd);
00079         
00080         printf("Hash : %d\n", j);
00081 
00082         return 0;
00083 }
